chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Constitution Petition No. D – 3794 of 2022 Date Order with signature of Judge FRESH CASE 1. For orders on Misc. No. 19435/2023. 2. For orders on office objection. 3. For orders on Misc. No. 17489/2022. 4. For hearing of Main Case. 06.09.2023: None for the petitioner. ------ Petitioner is called absent, no intimation is received, inspite of the fact that today, the matter is fixed in Court on an urgent application filed by the petitioner, who has chosen to remain absent without any intimation. It further appears that on 22.08.2022, petitioner was directed to provide complete details of plot, where commercial parking is to be started, however, needful has not been done, whereas, on 10.10.2022, further time was granted to the petitioner for such compliance, even thereafter, Court’s order has not been complied with. Perusal of memo of petition reflects that without showing any grievance, which could be redressed by this Court, while invoking its constitutional jurisdiction under Article 199 of the Constitution, appears to have been mentioned, whereas, the memo contains generalize and vague allegations. It has been further observed that same petitioner has filed large number of similar constitutional petitions in the garb of public interest litigation, however, without disclosing any cause of grievance and also based on generalize and vague allegations, which have been dismissed in limine by this Court, whereas, office has been directed to ensure that no such constitutional petitions in such format may be allowed to be entertained. 2 It appears that petitioner is in the habit of filing petitions against large number of Government officials, developers and builders just to cause harassment, as no specific grievance or violation of law, or any fundamental right has been shown, nor the petitioner, after filing such petitions, pursue the same, which reflects upon his malafide.

Perusal of memo of petition reflects that without showing any grievance, which could be redressed by this Court, while invoking its constitutional jurisdiction under Article 199 of the Constitution, appears to have been mentioned, whereas, the memo contains generalize and vague allegations. It has been further observed that same petitioner has filed large number of similar constitutional petitions in the garb of public interest litigation, however, without disclosing any cause of grievance and also based on generalize and vague allegations, which have been dismissed in limine by this Court, whereas, office has been directed to ensure that no such constitutional petitions in such format may be allowed to be entertained. 2 It appears that petitioner is in the habit of filing petitions against large number of Government officials, developers and builders just to cause harassment, as no specific grievance or violation of law, or any fundamental right has been shown, nor the petitioner, after filing such petitions, pursue the same, which reflects upon his malafide. Accordingly, instant petition being misconceived is dismissed alongwith listed / pending applications. JUDGE JUDGE AS/FA
